title: Networking Concepts
description: Compare TCP vs UDP, and explore packet loss, latency, jitter, and bandwidth optimization in game networking.
learningObjectives: Contrast TCP reliability with UDP high-speed packet delivery
Identify latency, packet loss, and jitter issues
Select appropriate protocols for game state synchronization (movement vs chat)

sections.title: TCP vs UDP for Real-time Games
sections.content:
Network protocols define communication pipelines:

1. **TCP**: Reliable, ordered, connection-oriented. Guarantees delivery by re-sending lost packets, but can block execution (Head-of-Line Blocking). Used for inventory updates or text chats.
2. **UDP**: Fast, unreliable, connectionless. Packs data and sends it immediately. Ideal for positional updates where old coordinates are useless and speed is paramount.

sections.title: Unreal Network Protocol Layer
sections.content: Unreal Engine packages replication state data on top of UDP sockets, implementing a custom reliability mechanism (reliable RPCs) to re-send critical packets without blocking general positional packets.
